whats_new.title: AI Vector Search
whats_new.description: Oracle AI Vector Search supports AI workloads by storing vector embeddings and querying data by semantic similarity inside Oracle Database.

whats_new.title: JSON-Relational Duality Views
whats_new.description: JSON-relational duality views let application data be accessed and updated as either JSON documents or relational tables.

whats_new.title: Property Graphs in SQL
whats_new.description: Oracle Database adds SQL property graph query support so graph relationships can be queried inside the database.

whats_new.title: Select AI enhancements
whats_new.description: Select AI extends natural-language database interaction and adds support for additional AI providers.

whats_new.title: Globally Distributed Database Raft replication
whats_new.description: Oracle Globally Distributed Database adds built-in Raft replication for distributed database deployments.

whats_new.title: SQL Firewall
whats_new.description: Oracle AI Database 26ai adds SQL Firewall capabilities to control which SQL can execute against the database.

whats_new.title: True Cache
whats_new.description: True Cache provides an automatically managed, consistent, mostly diskless cache for read-only workloads against Oracle AI Database.

whats_new.title: UCP asynchronous extension
whats_new.description: Universal Connection Pool adds asynchronous database-call support for scalable Java applications.

behavior_changes.title: Oracle Database release number changes
behavior_changes.description: The VERSION, VERSION_LEGACY, and VERSION_FULL release number values are updated in Oracle AI Database 26ai.

behavior_changes.title: SQL/JSON JSON_VALUE can return SQL BOOLEAN
behavior_changes.description: After COMPATIBLE is set to 23.0.0 or higher, SQL/JSON path item methods can return SQL BOOLEAN values.

behavior_changes.title: SYSDATE and SYSTIMESTAMP reflect PDB time zone
behavior_changes.description: SYSDATE and SYSTIMESTAMP can be managed separately for each database using the individual database time zone setting.

behavior_changes.title: Installation archives are digitally signed
behavior_changes.description: Oracle AI Database 26ai digitally signs installation archive files so deployments can verify package integrity.

behavior_changes.title: Version banner uses 26ai release numbering
behavior_changes.description: Oracle AI Database 26ai updates release-number reporting while retaining the underlying full version format used by the database.

deprecated.title: Oracle Enterprise Manager Database Express desupported
deprecated.description: Oracle Enterprise Manager Database Express is desupported in Oracle AI Database 26ai.

deprecated.title: Oracle Wallet Manager desupported
deprecated.description: Oracle Wallet Manager is desupported starting with Oracle AI Database 26ai.

deprecated.title: Traditional auditing desupported
deprecated.description: Traditional auditing is desupported in Oracle AI Database 26ai.

deprecated.title: DBUA and manual upgrade methods desupported
deprecated.description: AutoUpgrade and Replay Upgrade are the supported upgrade methods for Oracle AI Database 26ai; DBUA and manual upgrade methods are desupported.

deprecated.title: Oracle JDBC-OCI thick driver deprecated
deprecated.description: The Oracle JDBC OCI Type 2 client driver is deprecated in Oracle AI Database 26ai.

deprecated.title: RASADM desupported
deprecated.description: The Real Application Security GUI administration tool RASADM is desupported in Oracle AI Database 26ai; Oracle recommends using the RAS PL/SQL API.

deprecated.title: Legacy Oracle XML DB repository access paths reviewed
deprecated.description: Oracle AI Database 26ai upgrade guidance includes legacy XML DB and related component changes that require review during upgrade planning.

whats_new.title: Native JSON data type
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 21c introduces a SQL and PL/SQL JSON data type for binary JSON storage and direct client access.

whats_new.title: Blockchain tables
whats_new.description: Blockchain tables provide tamper-resistant, insert-only tables with retention controls for rows and table data.

whats_new.title: SQL macros
whats_new.description: SQL macros improve developer productivity by allowing PL/SQL functions to return SQL text that is expanded into a statement.

whats_new.title: JSON_TRANSFORM for JSON updates
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 21c adds SQL function JSON_TRANSFORM to update parts of a JSON document declaratively.

whats_new.title: Immutable tables
whats_new.description: Immutable tables protect inserted rows from update and deletion for a configured retention period.

behavior_changes.title: Read-only Oracle homes by default
behavior_changes.description: Oracle Database 21c configures Oracle Database homes in read-only mode by default, separating installation files from configuration and log files.

behavior_changes.title: Multitenant architecture required
behavior_changes.description: Starting with Oracle Database 21c, Oracle Database is only supported using the multitenant architecture.

behavior_changes.title: JSON data can use native binary storage
behavior_changes.description: Applications can store JSON in the native JSON data type, changing storage and client-access choices for JSON workloads.

deprecated.title: Traditional auditing deprecated
deprecated.description: Traditional auditing is deprecated in Oracle Database 21c; Oracle recommends using unified auditing.

deprecated.title: DBMS_OBFUSCATION_TOOLKIT desupported
deprecated.description: The DBMS_OBFUSCATION_TOOLKIT package is desupported in Oracle Database 21c and replaced with DBMS_CRYPTO.

deprecated.title: Oracle OLAP deprecated
deprecated.description: Analytic workspaces, the OLAP DML programming language, and the OLAP Java API are deprecated in Oracle Database 21c.

deprecated.title: Non-CDB architecture desupported
deprecated.description: Oracle Database 21c supports only the multitenant architecture, making non-CDB databases unavailable for this release line.

whats_new.title: Automatic indexing
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 19c can automatically create, rebuild, and drop indexes based on application workload changes.

whats_new.title: Real-time statistics
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 19c automatically gathers online statistics during conventional DML operations.

whats_new.title: SQL quarantine
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 19c can quarantine execution plans for SQL statements that consume excessive CPU or I/O resources.

whats_new.title: Hybrid partitioned tables
whats_new.description: Hybrid partitioned tables allow partitions to reside in both database segments and external files or sources.

whats_new.title: Memoptimized rowstore fast ingest
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 19c adds fast ingest support for high-frequency single-row inserts into the memoptimized rowstore.

behavior_changes.title: Oracle Database changes, desupports, and deprecations consolidated
behavior_changes.description: Oracle Database 19c upgrade documentation consolidates release behavior changes, deprecations, and desupport notices to help upgrade planning.

behavior_changes.title: Release update model replaces patch sets
behavior_changes.description: Oracle Database 19c upgrade guidance aligns with the release update model rather than older patch set delivery patterns.

deprecated.title: Oracle Text FILE_DATASTORE deprecated
deprecated.description: Starting with Oracle Database 19c, the Oracle Text FILE_DATASTORE type is deprecated; Oracle recommends DIRECTORY_DATASTORE.

deprecated.title: Oracle Text URL_DATASTORE deprecated
deprecated.description: Starting with Oracle Database 19c, the Oracle Text URL_DATASTORE type is deprecated; Oracle recommends NETWORK_DATASTORE.

deprecated.title: Oracle Streams desupported
deprecated.description: Oracle Streams is desupported in Oracle Database 19c upgrade planning guidance.

whats_new.title: Long identifiers
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 12c Release 2 increases the maximum length of most identifiers from 30 bytes to 128 bytes.

whats_new.title: JSON support enhancements
whats_new.description: Oracle Database 12c Release 2 adds JSON search and query improvements, including JSON data guide support.

whats_new.title: Online table redefinition improvements
whats_new.description: Online redefinition gains restart, rollback, progress monitoring, and broader table support.

whats_new.title: Online conversion to partitioned tables
whats_new.description: Nonpartitioned tables can be converted to partitioned tables online while indexes are maintained and DML continues.

whats_new.title: Online table move
whats_new.description: Nonpartitioned tables can be moved online without blocking concurrent DML, with automatic index maintenance as part of the move.

behavior_changes.title: Grid Infrastructure installation uses zip images
behavior_changes.description: Oracle Grid Infrastructure 12c Release 2 installation media is delivered as a zip image for the installer.

behavior_changes.title: 12.2 upgrade planning requires behavior-change review
behavior_changes.description: Oracle documents 12c Release 2 behavior changes separately from new features so upgrade projects can review compatibility impacts before moving forward.

deprecated.title: 12c Release 2 upgrade changes should be reviewed
deprecated.description: Oracle documents behavior changes, deprecated features, and desupported features for Oracle Database 12c Release 2 upgrade planning.

deprecated.title: Non-CDB architecture deprecated
deprecated.description: Oracle Database 12c Release 2 upgrade documentation identifies the non-CDB architecture as deprecated and directs users toward multitenant architecture planning.
